title: symphyotrichum shortii f. gronemannii
intro: Symphyotrichum shortii f. gronemannii, also known as the Short's Aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to the Great Plains region of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Symphyotrichum shortii f. gronemannii can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from respiratory issues.
